incidents.vector: Self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) targeting exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers, with wiper payload triggered by Iran timezone or Farsi locale
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on all Kubernetes cluster nodes or local machines matching Iranian locale. Leverages same infrastructure used in Trivy supply chain attack.

incidents.vector: Tycoon2FA adversary-in-the-middle phishing platform intercepts live authentication sessions to bypass MFA and steal session cookies
incidents.impact: Despite Europol seizing 330 domains on March 4, CrowdStrike confirms activity has resumed to pre-takedown levels. Platform previously responsible for 62% of phishing attempts blocked by Microsoft.

incidents.victim: Aqua Security Trivy vulnerability scanner users
incidents.vector: Supply chain attack injecting credential-stealing malware into official Trivy GitHub Actions releases
incidents.impact: Malicious versions exfiltrated SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and cryptocurrency wallets from users before Aqua Security removed the compromised files.

incidents.vector: DOJ seized four MOIS-controlled domains used for psychological operations, transnational repression, death threats via email, and doxing of targeted individuals
incidents.impact: Domains used to claim credit for hacks, post stolen PII of ~190 IDF-associated individuals, issue $250K bounty death threats against dissidents, and solicit cartel violence against targets in the US.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployment disrupting county government IT systems
incidents.impact: Phone lines and IT systems taken down across government offices serving nearly 600,000 residents. Medusa demanded $800,000 ransom.

incidents.victim: SocksEscort residential proxy network (369,000 infected IPs)
incidents.vector: Malware infected home and small business routers; access sold to cybercriminals who used it to mask fraud including bank account takeovers and unemployment insurance fraud
incidents.impact: International law enforcement dismantled the network. Victims include a NY crypto exchange customer ($1M loss), PA manufacturer ($700K), and US service members ($100K). Servers seized across Austria, France, and the Netherlands.

incidents.victim: IoT device owners and DDoS targets globally
incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised 3M+ IoT devices including routers and webcams to launch DDoS attacks and extort victims
incidents.impact: DOJ and international partners dismantled infrastructure. Aisuru alone issued 200,000+ attack commands. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Domains and servers seized.

incidents.attacker: Iran — state-directed hacktivist proxies (Handala, linked to MOIS)
incidents.vector: Compromised Microsoft Intune endpoint management system to issue mass remote wipe commands across Stryker's global network
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices, servers, and mobile phones wiped across 79 countries. 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland. Personal devices with Intune also wiped. CISA issued hardening advisory. DOJ seized Handala domains. FBI and CISA conducting enhanced response.

incidents.victim: Organizations using Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center
incidents.vector: Zero-day exploitation of CVE-2026-20131 in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center, exploited since January 26 before public disclosure on March 4
incidents.impact: Ransomware deployment with double extortion (data encryption plus regulatory threat). Interlock historically targets education, healthcare, government, and manufacturing. Previously attacked St. Paul MN, DaVita, and Ohio healthcare systems.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of multiple zero-day and known vulnerabilities in Cisco SD-WAN systems (7 CVEs), with two zero-days exploited for at least three years before discovery
incidents.impact: Five of nine disclosed vulnerabilities confirmed actively exploited. Compromising SD-WAN gives attackers access to policy, routing, segmentation, and administrative trust over enterprise networks. CISA issued emergency directive.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployment shutting down hospital IT systems
incidents.impact: Hospital dark for 9 days. All 35 clinic locations closed. Cancer infusion patients rescheduled. Mississippi's only Level I trauma center, children's hospital, and organ transplant programs disrupted. Medusa demanded $800,000 ransom and threatened data leak.

incidents.vector: Custom backdoors (AppleChris, MemFun), PowerShell reverse shells to C2 servers, credential harvesting via Getpass and Mimikatz, WMI lateral movement through unmanaged endpoints
incidents.impact: Selective intelligence collection targeting military capability files, joint military activities, organizational structures, and C4I systems. Campaign active since at least 2020 with renewed activity in 2026.

incidents.victim: Marquis Software and 74+ banks and financial institutions
incidents.vector: Breach of bank software vendor that provides customer relationship management tools to financial institutions
incidents.impact: 672,075 people confirmed affected; researchers estimate up to 1.35 million. Stolen data includes SSNs, financial account info, dates of birth, and addresses. Marquis reportedly paid a ransom. Multiple banks issued breach notifications.
